mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 7.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and imminent recession, triggering immediate risk-off positioning that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal fails to predict direction with > 55% accuracy over a 5-year out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and monthly auto sales confirms robust consumer spending momentum, which directly translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or forward quarterly returns are not statistically higher than the market median when both series trend upward.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase indicates supply chain constraints and margin compression, which forces earnings downgrades and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances within late-cycle regimes, or the predictive accuracy drops below 50% in a walk-forward test restricted to late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer price inflation combined with an artificially low policy rate compresses real yields, forcing the central bank to initiate aggressive tightening that rapidly discounts equity valuations and causes negative forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL crosses above 225 AND FEDFUNDS crosses below 0.5
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample predictive power over single-series models, or 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of triggered observations over a 5-year horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal immediate construction sector activity and household wealth effects, which generate positive short-term equity momentum through increased consumer spending and financial sector earnings.
failure_condition: The signal yields a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher than zero (t-test p > 0.05) when housing starts trend upward.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in nonfarm payrolls crossing below -50,000 positions confirms labor market contraction, which reduces aggregate demand and forces corporate earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% in a walk-forward test over any 3-year out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, synchronized increases in auto sales and industrial production confirm demand-supply alignment, eliminating inventory drag and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS crosses above 400000 AND INDPRO crosses above 95
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in > 55% of triggered instances within post-shock recovery regimes, or the interaction effect shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.
